summary: Unit tests (SecretRotationValidator.Tests.ps1) are well-structured with good coverage of core functions (Get-SecretStatus, Get-SecretRotationReport, Format-RotationReport, CLI script), edge cases (expiring today, empty input, configurable warning window), and error scenarios (missing fields, bad JSON, missing config). However, the end-to-end test harness (Run-ActTests.ps1) references fixture files at tests/fixtures/{mixed,all-ok,empty}/secrets.json that are not provided, preventing Run-ActTests.ps1 from executing—this is a critical blocker since the test harness copies these fixtures to temp directories before running `act`. Additionally, the -FailOnExpired flag in Invoke-SecretRotationValidator.ps1 is not tested, and some input validation edge cases (invalid dates, negative rotation policy) are missing. With fixture files provided and -FailOnExpired covered, the test suite would be comprehensive.
